steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks which choice uses the notes to explain the advantages of onshore wind farms over offshore ones. The relevant notes are: offshore farms produce more electricity but are more expensive to build and maintain. A correct answer must state an onshore advantage and explicitly compare the two types.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Though they don't produce as much electricity as offshore wind farms, onshore farms are usually cheaper to build and maintain.: Directly compares the two types and names the onshore advantage (lower cost) while honestly acknowledging the trade-off (less electricity). Effectively explains the advantage. ✓
  • Choice B — Rajasthan, India, is home to Jaisalmer Wind Park.: States only a geographic fact about one farm. Makes no comparison between onshore and offshore. ✗
  • Choice C — Like other wind farms, Jaisalmer Wind Park in Rajasthan, India, uses turbines to turn wind power into electricity.: Describes how wind farms work in general. Makes no comparison between onshore and offshore. ✗
  • Choice D — Whether onshore or offshore, wind farms harness the power of wind to generate electricity.: Treats both types as equivalent. Identifies no advantage of one over the other. ✗
That is the correct answer.
